headlines.title: Sturgeon tells BBC: I'm serving a sentence for crime I didn't commit
headlines.description: Scotland's former first minister is challenged by Laura Kuenssberg over the SNP embezzlement scandal, and at times struggles to hold back tears.

headlines.title: Watch: Key moments from Nicola Sturgeon's interview with Laura Kuenssberg
headlines.description: Former First Minister of Scotland Nicola Sturgeon speaks to the BBC in her first interview since her estranged husband Peter Murrell pled guilty to embezzling £400,000 from the SNP

headlines.title: Hundreds arrested in France after wild Champions League celebrations
headlines.description: Thousands of officers tried to quell unrest as crowds of fans - some firing flares - celebrated PSG's victory over Arsenal.

headlines.title: How Putin became master of the image
headlines.description: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

headlines.title: Why Britain's notoriously bad train wi-fi might soon be a thing of the past
headlines.description: How bad is the signal on our trains? We spoke to commuters to find out.

headlines.title: Killer fungus could be good news for habitats decimated by invasive moss
headlines.description: A newly discovered species raises hope that some native British habitats could be restored.

headlines.title: 'Almost rage bait': Has Euphoria gone from defining Gen Z to dividing them?
headlines.description: Season three ends on Monday and many of the show's original fans say they have "outgrown" the drama.

headlines.title: Tip Toe's creator Russell T Davies: 'I wish television could change the world'
headlines.description: BBC News given exclusive behind-the-scenes access on set of Tip Toe.

headlines.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
headlines.description: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.

headlines.title: Inside Australia’s mouse nightmare: The farmers living with a rodent plague
headlines.description: Farmers report thousands of mice per hectare destroying crops and invading homes.

headlines.title: Why renaming my health condition could help other women
headlines.description: Rochelle Lewis is among more than 170 million woman globally to be diagnosed with PCOS.

headlines.title: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
headlines.description: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

headlines.title: Streeting suggests NI cut and North Sea drilling
headlines.description: The former health secretary has been distancing himself from policies pursued by Sir Keir Starmer's government.

headlines.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
headlines.description: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

headlines.title: Oscar-winning Star Wars editor Marcia Lucas dies aged 80
headlines.description: The film editor and ex-wife of director George Lucas was widely recognised as a pivotal creative force behind the original space trilogy.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Agony for Arsenal' and '5 cops axed' at Kensington Palace
headlines.description: Several of the papers focus on Arsenal's Champions League loss to Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday.

headlines.title: 'I started working here at 15 - now I'm retiring after 75 years'
headlines.description: Sadie Jefferson started work at the pharmacy, aged 15, back in 1951 and has worked there for 75 years.

headlines.title: Rare ancient rainforest in Northern Ireland set to come back to life
headlines.description: Ulster Wildlife takes on 100 year restoration project of ancient rainforest.

headlines.title: Metallica urge fans to step-up and donate blood ahead of UK tour
headlines.description: US heavy metal band join call for more UK blood donors ahead of their concert in Cardiff this June.

headlines.title: I invited guests to play with a bomb in my garden
headlines.description: Valerie Smith refused to leave when a gardener found a bomb in her garden.

headlines.title: Biker's sidecar-riding dog 'making people smile'
headlines.description: Louis the St Bernard enjoys riding around town, and people often stop to take photos, his owner says.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Nicola Sturgeon speaks to the BBC
headlines.description: Laura sits down with the former first minister and SNP leader

headlines.title: Does the US need new laws to protect elections?
headlines.description: And, why does Donald Trump want to “nationalise” elections?

headlines.title: Michael Caine and Glenda Jackson star in a heart-warming tale
headlines.description: WWII veteran Bernard sneaks out of his care home to attend the 70th anniversary of D-Day

headlines.title: A powerful film about the rise and tragic fall of a wrestling family
headlines.description: Based on a true story of brothers who made history in the world of professional wrestling.

headlines.title: Fletcher chosen to replace Gilmour in Scotland squad
headlines.description: Manchester United teenager Tyler Fletcher is a late replacement for the injured Billy Gilmour in Scotland's World Cup squad.

headlines.title: 'He wanted to take it' - Gabriel's first Arsenal penalty ends with heartbreak
headlines.description: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.

headlines.title: PSG go back-to-back and join 'greatest of all time'
headlines.description: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

headlines.title: Slot sacking completes a remarkable fall from grace
headlines.description: 'Slot's is one of the most remarkable falls from grace in Premier League history' says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: PSG retain Champions League with penalty shootout win over Arsenal
headlines.description: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

headlines.title: 32 and counting - why are women overlooked for French Open night matches?
headlines.description: All eight of the French Open's night sessions this year have been allocated to men.
